intro: Peucedanum rivae, also known as Rivaz's hog's fennel, is a plant species within the carrot family (Apiaceae) that is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including the reduction of inflammation and improvement of digestive issues due to its antispasmodic and carminative effects. The bioactive constituents of Peucedanum rivae include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Peucedanum rivae is typically used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including gastrointestinal disorders and respiratory issues.
